title: flaveria haumanii
intro: Flaveria haumanii is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family and commonly known as Queñoa or Queñua. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Flaveria haumanii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antidiabetic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions made from the leaves or roots, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
